title: Burgers flux of a constant state is constant

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a constant grid state c, the Burgers flux at every index is c squared over two.
assumptions: i is a natural-number grid index
the grid state is constant with value c
informal.expected_conclusion: The Burgers flux of a constant state is c squared over two.

declarations.name: burgersFlux
declarations.statement: def burgersFlux (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := (u i) ^ 2 / 2

declarations.name: burgersFlux_const
declarations.statement: theorem burgersFlux_const (c : Real) (i : Nat) : burgersFlux (fun _ => c) i = c ^ 2 / 2

tasks.input: Translate this Burgers conservative finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: For a constant grid state c, the Burgers flux at every index is c squared over two.
tasks.target: theorem burgersFlux_const (c : Real) (i : Nat) : burgersFlux (fun _ => c) i = c ^ 2 / 2

tasks.input: Given the Burgers scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding burgersFlux and using ring.
